heading: Opportunity to obtain witnesses and other evidence
label: 949j Opportunity to obtain witnesses and other evidence
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 949j, added , , , related to the opportunity to obtain witnesses and other evidence, prior to the general amendment of this chapter by .Pub. L. 109–366, § 3(a)(1)120 Stat. 2614Pub. L. 111–84Oct. 17, 2006
amendments: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 949j, added , , , related to the opportunity to obtain witnesses and other evidence, prior to the general amendment of this chapter by .Pub. L. 109–366, § 3(a)(1)120 Stat. 2614Pub. L. 111–84Oct. 17, 2006

children.text: Defense counsel in a military commission under this chapter shall have a reasonable opportunity to obtain witnesses and other evidence as provided in regulations prescribed by the Secretary of Defense. The opportunity to obtain witnesses and evidence shall be comparable to the opportunity available to a criminal defendant in a court of the United States under article III of the Constitution.

children.label: A
children.text: shall be similar to that which courts of the United States having criminal jurisdiction may lawfully issue; and

children.label: B
children.text: shall run to any place where the United States shall have jurisdiction thereof.

children.label: A
children.text: negate the guilt of the accused of an offense charged; or

children.label: B
children.text: reduce the degree of guilt of the accused with respect to an offense charged.

The trial counsel shall, as soon as practicable, disclose to the defense the existence of evidence that reasonably tends to impeach the credibility of a witness whom the government intends to call at trial.

The trial counsel shall, as soon as practicable upon a finding of guilt, disclose to the defense the existence of evidence that is not subject to paragraph (1) or paragraph (2) but that reasonably may be viewed as mitigation evidence at sentencing.

The disclosure obligations under this subsection encompass evidence that is known or reasonably should be known to any government officials who participated in the investigation and prosecution of the case against the defendant.
